message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "payout is limited to remainingCap (second claim returns 600 G when only 600 left)"
Passing tests so far: 25

Spec: Total payout is capped at remainingCap. If desired payout exceeds remainingCap, the actual payout is clamped to remainingCap.

Test: damages amount 1500, sword ench 3 (standard), normally 1400 payout, but remainingCap is only 600 → actual payout 600, new remainingCap 0.

Working directory: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking
Run tests with: pnpm test

After computing total payout, clamp it to min(payout, policy.remainingCap). Update remainingCap accordingly.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current state of the implementation and test files, then make the minimal change needed to pass the failing test.
Now I'll make the minimal change to clamp the payout to `min(payout, policy.remainingCap)`.

content.text:
All tests pass (26 passing, up from 25).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added `payout = Math.min(payout, input.policy.remainingCap);` before computing the new `remainingCap`.
- Result: All tests now pass (26 passing).
- Approach: Minimal one-line addition that clamps the computed payout to the remaining cap, exactly as the spec requires. No refactoring, no other changes.
